mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings forecasts higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change on forward 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0%, it signals a weakening labor market that reduces aggregate demand and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns after UNRATE crosses above 4.0% are not negative in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising policy rates and high inflation indicate aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations via higher discount rates and lower earnings growth expectations, driving negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 4% and FEDFUNDS > previous quarter average
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns during jointly triggered periods are not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05 against zero).

mechanism_narrative: In the early recovery phase after a recession trough, stronger-than-expected industrial production growth signals a durable expansion, surprising markets and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% after NBER-dated recession trough
failure_condition: The positive return effect during post-recession periods is not significantly larger than during expansions (difference-in-means p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls confirms labor market strength, raising disposable income and aggregate demand, which supports corporate profits and produces positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of payrolls change on forward 5-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarterly GDP growth rate signals an imminent or current recession, sharply reducing earnings expectations and risk appetite, leading to a sustained decline in the S&P 500 over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns after negative GDP growth are not negative in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based demand collapse that precedes earnings disappointments and falling equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 5% and RSAFS month-over-month decline > 2%
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns after joint trigger are not negative in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment, any policy rate increase signals premature removal of accommodation, shocking markets and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as reflation trades unwind.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.5 and increases by at least 0.25% in the prior month
failure_condition: The negative return effect of rate hikes near zero is not significantly different from rate hikes in normal years (difference-in-differences p > 0.10).
